message:
fix(collect): improve robustness of proc filesystem parsing and network collection

Multiple defensive improvements to the collection layer:

1. Tick Delta - Monotonic Timestamps (tick_delta.rs):
   - Add TickSnapshot.monotonic: Option<Instant> field for precise timing
   - compute_tick_delta() prefers monotonic time over SystemTime
   - Prevents false negatives from wall clock adjustments (NTP, DST, etc.)
   - Falls back to SystemTime when monotonic not available

2. FD Parser - Defensive Parsing (proc_parsers.rs):
   - parse_fd_dir() now validates FD numbers are numeric before processing
   - Non-numeric entries (e.g., "foo") are silently skipped
   - Prevents fd=0 being recorded for garbage filenames
   - Fixes edge case where malformed /proc/pid/fd entries cause confusion

3. Network Collection - O(1) Lookup (network.rs):
   - NEW NetworkSnapshot struct: indexes all sockets by inode
   - collect_network_info() now uses NetworkSnapshot internally
   - For bulk collection, use NetworkSnapshot::collect() once, then
     get_process_info() per process - avoids O(N*M) /proc/net/* reads
   - Unix socket path fix: join remaining parts for paths with spaces
     (e.g., "/run/user/1000/bus socket" now parsed correctly)

4. Session Parser - Bounds Checking (session.rs):
   - ProcStat::from_stat_content() uses .get() instead of slicing
   - Prevents panic on malformed /proc/pid/stat content
   - Returns None gracefully when comm field parsing fails

5. Real Tests - Lint Fixes (real_tests.rs):
   - Remove unused mut warnings from ProcessHandle bindings

These changes improve reliability when parsing edge-case /proc content
and significantly improve performance for network collection at scale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
